summary: Governed sequencing rules for procedure assembly inside a rendered page.

ordering_rules.rule: all preconditions must be presented before the first numbered step
any warning whose target step is irreversible MUST appear immediately before that step, not at end of document
warnings with `must_be_attached_to_kinds` MUST be inlined into every matching kind's procedure block
escalation guidance MUST appear at the failure-edge of a step (after expected observation), never aggregated at end
symptoms -> diagnostic_questions -> next_actions -> escalation; reverse ordering is forbidden
if procedure A is a precondition of procedure B, A MUST be linked from B's preconditions block (not silently assumed)
every irreversible step MUST have an associated recovery_path or explicit `no_recovery_possible: true` flag
warnings without a target procedure MUST NOT appear in the main flow; they belong in the warnings index page
a prerequisite revealed mid-procedure is a sequencing defect; it must be promoted to preconditions

warning_placement_policy.P0-irreversible-or-safety-blocking: inline immediately before triggering step; visually distinct
warning_placement_policy.P2-operational: inline before triggering step; may collapse if repeated within same procedure

escalation_insertion_policy.format_rule: single-line escalation cue with channel reference; no embedded narrative

recovery_instruction_policy.rule: recovery instructions appear with the failure they recover from, not in a separate recovery section
recovery_instruction_policy.exception: system-wide reset paths may have a dedicated section AND inline reference
